title: WOGONIN
description: Wogonin is a natural flavonoid compound extracted from Scutellaria baicalensis (Chinese skullcap) and related species. It functions primarily through anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidative, and anti-cancer mechanisms, including modulation of MAPK signaling pathways, inhibition of COX-2 expression, and induction of ferroptosis in cancer cells. Research suggests potential applications in cancer treatment, pulmonary conditions, and inflammatory diseases.

research.title: Wogonin attenuates septic cardiomyopathy by suppressing ALOX15-mediated ferroptosis
research.summary: Wogonin protected against septic cardiomyopathy in mice by directly binding to and inhibiting ALOX15, preventing cardiomyocyte ferroptosis and improving cardiac function. The cardioprotective effects were mediated through suppression of ALOX15-initiated ferroptosis.

research.title: Wogonin Attenuates Bleomycin-Induced Pulmonary Fibrosis and Oxidative Stress Injury via the MAPK Signaling Pathway
research.summary: Wogonin significantly reduced pulmonary fibrosis and oxidative stress in a rat model by modulating the MAPK pathway, showing potential as a therapeutic agent for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis.

research.title: Wogonin, a bioactive flavonoid in herbal tea, inhibits inflammatory cyclooxygenase-2 gene expression in human lung epithelial cancer cells
research.summary: Wogonin inhibited PMA-induced COX-2 expression in lung cancer cells at the transcriptional level through the MEK1/2 pathway and AP-1 activity, demonstrating anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties.

research.title: Antiproliferative Wnt inhibitor wogonin prevents eryptosis following ionophoric challenge, hyperosmotic shock, oxidative stress, and metabolic deprivation
research.summary: Wogonin protected red blood cells from premature death (eryptosis) under various stress conditions including calcium overload and oxidative stress, suggesting potential in preventing chemotherapy-related anemia.

research.title: Detection of baicalin metabolites baicalein and oroxylin-a in mouse pancreas and pancreatic xenografts
research.summary: Study demonstrated that baicalin from Scutellaria baicalensis undergoes significant methylation to oroxylin A during absorption, with substantial amounts detected in pancreatic tissue and tumors, showing tissue-specific distribution patterns.

research.title: Comparison of the major flavonoid content of S. baicalensis, S. lateriflora, and their commercial products
research.summary: Analysis of Scutellaria species found significant variations in wogonin and baicalin content between roots and aerial parts, with roots containing substantially higher levels of active flavonoids.

sentiment.summary: Limited direct user experiences with wogonin as a standalone supplement. Most User feedback discussions focus on other compounds or broader herbal formulations containing Scutellaria species, with minimal specific feedback on wogonin's effects or tolerability.

effects.label: Cancer Applications:
effects.description: Shows promising results in preclinical studies for pancreatic and lung cancer through ferroptosis induction and PI3K/AKT pathway modulation

effects.label: Pulmonary Conditions:
effects.description: Demonstrates effectiveness in reducing pulmonary fibrosis and inflammation in animal models

effects.label: Cardiac Protection:
effects.description: Provides significant protection against septic cardiomyopathy in experimental settings

effects.label: Limited Human Data:
effects.description: Most evidence comes from in vitro and animal studies; clinical human trials are lacking

dosage.label: Animal Studies:
dosage.description: Doses of 20-60 mg/kg administered intraperitoneally in mouse models showed cardioprotective and anti-fibrotic effects

dosage.label: Cell Culture:
dosage.description: Concentrations of 50-100 μM used in cancer cell studies to induce ferroptosis and inhibit proliferation

dosage.label: Human Dosing:
dosage.description: No standardized human dosing guidelines established; typically consumed as part of Scutellaria baicalensis extracts in traditional medicine

dosage.label: Bioavailability:
dosage.description: Undergoes significant methylation to oroxylin A during absorption, affecting tissue distribution and metabolite activity

sideEffects.label: Limited Safety Data:
sideEffects.description: Few reported side effects in research studies, but long-term human safety data is minimal

sideEffects.label: Interaction Potential:
sideEffects.description: As a COX-2 inhibitor and MAPK modulator, may interact with medications affecting these pathways

sideEffects.label: Metabolic Considerations:
sideEffects.description: Converts to oroxylin A metabolite which may have different activity profile than parent compound

sideEffects.label: Red Blood Cell Effects:
sideEffects.description: While protective against eryptosis in some conditions, effects on normal red blood cell function require further study

availability.label: Herbal Sources:
availability.description: Available primarily through Scutellaria baicalensis (Chinese skullcap) root extracts and supplements

availability.label: Standardization Issues:
availability.description: Content varies significantly between products and plant parts (roots vs. aerial parts), with roots containing higher concentrations

availability.label: Research Compounds:
availability.description: Pure wogonin available as research chemical but not commonly sold as standalone dietary supplement
